evals.name: gtm-saas-expansion
evals.prompt:
Start discovery for the HealthBridge engagement. The project file is at evals/fixtures/gtm-saas-expansion/diamond-project.json. Plugin research and competitive outputs already exist in the discover/ subdirectories — use them as pre-completed plugin method outputs. For guided methods, I want customer journey analysis. Here are my responses for the customer journey:

- Primary journey: Mid-market clinic administrator evaluating and purchasing a practice management platform
- Key stages: Problem awareness (current tools failing) → Vendor research (Google, peer referrals, GPO catalogs) → Shortlist (3-4 vendors, demo requests) → Evaluation (pilot with 1 department, 4-6 weeks) → Decision (committee of 3-5, compliance sign-off required) → Onboarding (IT integration, staff training, 4-8 weeks)
- Pain points: Vendor demos don't reflect real clinic workflows; compliance review adds 2-3 months; IT integration with existing EHR is the #1 deal-breaker; staff resistance to change during onboarding
- Drop-off points: 40% drop between demo and pilot (too complex to set up); 25% drop at compliance review (can't prove HIPAA/GDPR readiness)
- Emotional journey: Frustrated (current tools) → Hopeful (new vendor) → Overwhelmed (evaluation complexity) → Anxious (compliance risk) → Relieved (successful pilot) → Cautiously optimistic (go-live)

Synthesize all discovery findings and assess readiness for the Define phase.
evals.expected_output: Discovery synthesis with 5-10 themes integrating desk research, competitive baseline, and customer journey. Themes specific to mid-market healthcare GTM. At least 1 surprise and 1 tension. Phase transition assessment.
expectations: discover/synthesis.md exists and contains ## Themes, ## Surprises, and ## Tensions sections (or equivalent headers)
Synthesis contains 5-10 distinct themes (not categories — each theme states a specific insight)
At least 1 surprise that challenges the engagement's starting assumption about mid-market healthcare
At least 1 tension that articulates a genuine trade-off (not 'more research needed')
Customer journey map saved to discover/customer-journey.md with stages, pain points, and drop-offs
Synthesis references evidence from both desk research AND competitive baseline AND customer journey (triangulation)
Phase state updated via update-phase.sh (discover set to in-progress or complete)
Synthesis includes a phase transition assessment (ready for Define, or what's missing)
Each theme has an explicit 'so what' or implication statement

evals.name: digital-transform-manufacturing
evals.prompt:
Lass uns die Problemlandschaft für das SmartFactory-Engagement erkunden. Die Projektdatei liegt unter evals/fixtures/digital-transform-mfg/diamond-project.json. Plugin-Outputs für Desk Research, Trends und Wettbewerb existieren bereits in den discover/ Unterverzeichnissen. Bitte führe zusätzlich beide geführten Methoden durch: Stakeholder Mapping und Data Audit.

Für Stakeholder Mapping, meine Antworten:
- Entscheidungsträger: Thomas Weber (Werksleiter, finale Budgetfreigabe), Dr. Petra Schneider (CTO, Technologieauswahl), Klaus Richter (CFO, ROI-Verantwortung)
- Direkt betroffen: 12 Schichtleiter (Fertigungslinien 1-4), 45 CNC-Bediener, 8 Instandhalter, IT-Abteilung (3 Personen, aktuell überlastet)
- Relevante Expertise: Prof. Hartmann (Fraunhofer IPT, Berater für Retrofit), Maria Gonzalez (Siemens-Vertriebspartnerin, kennt die S7-Landschaft)
- Blockierer: Betriebsratsvorsitzende Andrea Müller (besorgt über Leistungsüberwachung), IT-Leiter Jens Bauer (fürchtet Kontrollverlust an Cloud-Anbieter)
- Externe: Siemens (Maschinenlieferant, Wartungsverträge), TÜV Süd (Zertifizierung), Stadtwerke Sindelfingen (Energiemonitoring)

Für Data Audit:
- Verfügbare Daten: SAP ERP (Auftrags- und Materialdaten, gut gepflegt), Siemens S7-Steuerungsdaten (nur lokal auf Maschinen, kein zentraler Zugriff), Excel-basierte Qualitätsprotokolle (pro Schicht, inkonsistentes Format), Papierbasierte Wartungsprotokolle (Instandhaltung), Energieverbrauch über Stadtwerke-API (monatlich, 15min-Intervalle)
- Datenqualität: SAP-Daten zuverlässig, S7-Daten existieren aber sind nicht zugänglich ohne Retrofit, Qualitätsdaten haben 30% Lücken an Wochenenden, Wartungsdaten nicht digitalisiert
- Kritische Lücken: Keine Echtzeit-Maschinendaten, kein zentrales Datenmodell, keine Verbindung zwischen Auftrags- und Maschinendaten

Bitte synthetisiere alle Entdeckungen und bewerte die Bereitschaft für die Define-Phase. Kommunikationssprache ist Deutsch.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive German-language discovery synthesis integrating all 5 methods (3 plugin + 2 guided). Stakeholder map with influence/interest matrix. Data audit with gap analysis. Synthesis themes in German, technical terms in English. Phase transition assessment.
expectations: discover/synthesis.md exists with section headers in German
discover/stakeholder-map.md exists with influence/interest matrix containing all named stakeholders
discover/data-audit.md exists with data inventory table and gap analysis
Synthesis themes integrate findings from at least 3 different methods (not single-source themes)
German prose used for all non-technical content; English preserved for technical terms (OPC UA, Edge Computing, etc.)
Betriebsrat concerns explicitly addressed in stakeholder analysis (not a generic stakeholder)
Data audit identifies the S7 accessibility gap as a critical blocker for the engagement
Phase state management attempted via update-phase.sh

evals.name: cost-opt-thin-data
evals.prompt:
Run discovery on the CostSmart engagement. Project file at evals/fixtures/cost-opt-thin-data/diamond-project.json. Only desk research has plugin output — it's in discover/research/summary-thin.md and it returned thin results (only 2 findings). No competitive or trend analysis was run. Please execute stakeholder mapping and data audit as guided methods.

For stakeholder mapping:
- Decision-makers: Sarah Chen (CIO, owns IT budget), Marcus Weber (COO, operational efficiency mandate), compliance officer role is vacant (interim handled by external auditor from KPMG)
- Affected: IT operations team (42 staff across 3 data centers), branch IT support (12 regional coordinators), vendor management team (3 people managing 28 vendor contracts)
- External: AWS (current primary cloud vendor), Deutsche Telekom (network infrastructure), KPMG (interim compliance)
- Potential blockers: Works council (any restructuring needs approval), branch managers (fear losing local IT support)

For data audit:
- Available: ServiceNow ITSM (incident data, well-structured, 3 years history), AWS Cost Explorer (cloud spend, granular), SAP FI (financial data, reliable), vendor contract database (Excel, manually maintained, some gaps)
- Quality: ITSM data is good but incident categories are inconsistent across data centers; AWS data excellent; financial data reliable but IT costs bundled with facilities in some branches; vendor contracts have 15% missing renewal dates
- Critical gaps: No unified view of total IT spend (split across 4 systems); no baseline for cost-per-service or cost-per-user metrics; branch-level IT costs not separated from central

Synthesize findings. I expect the skill to handle the thin desk research gracefully — note the gaps, don't fabricate.
evals.expected_output: Discovery synthesis that handles thin plugin results transparently. Relies on guided methods to compensate. Explicit gap acknowledgment. Fewer themes acceptable given thin input. No fabricated findings.
expectations: discover/synthesis.md explicitly flags that desk research returned thin results (only 2 findings)
Synthesis does NOT fabricate additional findings to fill the research gap
Absence of competitive analysis explicitly noted (cogni-portfolio was not dispatched)
Synthesis compensates by leaning on stakeholder and data audit insights
Theme count is appropriately modest (3-7 given thin input — not inflated to 10)
Consultant offered option to retry desk research with adjusted parameters or broader scope
discover/stakeholder-map.md captures the vacant compliance officer role as a risk factor
discover/data-audit.md identifies the fragmented IT cost visibility as the #1 gap
Synthesis acknowledges reduced confidence due to limited evidence triangulation
